summary.text: The dominant pattern today is a surge of interest in making AI agents cheaper and more capable. Headroom, which compresses tool outputs and logs by 60–95% before LLM consumption, gained 12.7k stars in a single day, the highest delta among all tracked projects. This suggests developers are acutely feeling token costs in production agent workflows. Simultaneously, Agent Reach (1.7k daily stars) offers zero-fee access to social and code platforms via MCP, addressing another pain point: API costs and integration friction. The sustained hot projects addyosani/agent-skills and esengine/DeepSeek-Reasonix continue to grow steadily, reinforcing that agent skill packs and terminal-native coding agents remain in demand. The absence of any new breakout in fine-tuning or RAG categories is notable, as attention concentrates on agent efficiency and tool access layers.

projects.name: Agent-Reach
projects.description: Give your AI agent eyes to see the entire internet. Read & search Twitter, Reddit, YouTube, GitHub, Bilibili, XiaoHongShu — one CLI, zero API fees.

llmSummary.oneLiner: Agent Reach is a CLI tool that gives AI coding agents zero-friction access to read and search across Twitter, Reddit, YouTube, GitHub, Bilibili, Xiaohongshu, and more — without any API fees.
llmSummary.problemSolved: AI agents can write code but struggle to fetch real-time web content due to paywalled APIs, login walls, and anti-bot measures. Agent Reach bundles pre-configured, multi-backend channels for each platform, handling installation, authentication, and fallback routing so agents can read tweets, search Reddit, extract YouTube transcripts, and browse Chinese platforms with a single command.
llmSummary.whyNow: With 26,660 stars and a 7-day average of 800 new stars per day, Agent Reach is experiencing sustained, high-velocity growth. The 24-hour delta of 597 and acceleration of 0.7 indicate a steady, organic surge — not a one-day spike. This momentum suggests broad, ongoing adoption among AI developers who need their agents to interact with the open web.
llmSummary.whatChanged: No single obvious trigger — the project appears to be sustaining organic growth. The README was recently updated (pushed June 12, 2026) with expanded platform support and a new 'doctor' diagnostic command, but the star velocity has been consistently high for over a week, indicating word-of-mouth and viral spread within the AI agent community.
llmSummary.whyTrending: It solves a universal pain point for AI agent users: the hassle of configuring API keys, handling rate limits, and bypassing blocks for each web platform. By offering a zero-config, free, and open-source solution that works with Claude Code, Cursor, OpenClaw, and Windsurf, it has become a must-have tool for anyone building agentic workflows that require live web data.
llmSummary.developerTakeaway: Agent Reach demonstrates that the next frontier for AI agents is not just coding but autonomous web interaction. Developers should consider integrating similar multi-backend, self-healing channel architectures into their own agent toolchains to reduce friction and improve reliability.
llmSummary.developerInsight: This signals a shift toward 'agent infrastructure' as a new category: tools that abstract away platform-specific barriers (API costs, login requirements, anti-bot measures) so agents can operate freely. The sustained star growth indicates strong product-market fit and a clear demand for open-source, community-maintained alternatives to paid data APIs.
llmSummary.targetUsers: AI developers and power users who run Claude Code, Cursor, OpenClaw, Windsurf, or any CLI-based agent and need their agent to read/search Twitter, Reddit, YouTube, GitHub, Bilibili, Xiaohongshu, LinkedIn, RSS, and the general web without paying for API keys or writing custom scrapers.
llmSummary.riskNotes: Relies on third-party CLI tools and browser cookies for some platforms (Twitter, Xiaohongshu, Reddit), which may break if those tools are abandoned or platforms change their anti-bot measures. Cookie-based authentication carries account ban risk — users are advised to use throwaway accounts. The project's long-term maintenance depends on the author's continued engagement.

projects.name: headroom
projects.description: Compress tool outputs, logs, files, and RAG chunks before they reach the LLM. 60-95% fewer tokens, same answers. Library, proxy, MCP server.

llmSummary.oneLiner: Headroom compresses tool outputs, logs, files, and RAG chunks by 60–95% before they reach the LLM, preserving answer quality while slashing token costs.
llmSummary.problemSolved: AI agents consume massive token budgets on verbose tool outputs, logs, and context, leading to high costs and context window overflow. Headroom reduces token usage without sacrificing accuracy, and works as a library, proxy, or MCP server.
llmSummary.whyNow: With 26,586 stars and a 24-hour delta of 11,923, Headroom is experiencing explosive growth. The acceleration ratio is 1 (low), indicating sustained hot momentum rather than a sudden spike. This level of attention signals a massive unmet need for token optimization in the AI agent ecosystem.
llmSummary.whatChanged: No single obvious trigger — the project appears to be sustaining organic growth. The recent release and active commits suggest ongoing development, but the star surge likely stems from viral word-of-mouth among AI developers frustrated with token costs.
llmSummary.whyTrending: Headroom addresses a universal pain point for anyone using AI agents: token costs and context limits. Its multi-mode deployment (library, proxy, MCP) and proven 60-95% savings with no accuracy loss make it an immediate practical win. The cross-agent memory and reversible compression add unique value.
llmSummary.developerTakeaway: If you use Claude Code, Cursor, Codex, or any LLM agent, Headroom can cut your token bill by 60-95% with zero code changes via its proxy mode. It's a no-brainer drop-in optimization.
llmSummary.developerInsight: This signals a shift toward 'context engineering' as a first-class concern in AI development. Tools that optimize token usage without degrading quality will become essential infrastructure, much like caching layers for databases.
llmSummary.targetUsers: AI developers and teams using coding agents (Claude Code, Cursor, Codex, Aider, Copilot), RAG pipelines, or any LLM-based application where token costs or context limits are a bottleneck.
llmSummary.riskNotes: The project is very new (26k stars in a short time) and may face scalability or maintenance challenges. The 234 open issues suggest active development but also potential rough edges. Reversible compression adds complexity and may not be needed for all use cases.

projects.name: ai-engineering-from-scratch
projects.description: Learn it. Build it. Ship it for others.

llmSummary.oneLiner: A massive, free, open-source curriculum (503 lessons, 20 phases) that teaches AI engineering from math foundations to production agents, with every lesson producing a reusable artifact.
llmSummary.problemSolved: Most AI learning resources are scattered, shallow, or API-focused. This curriculum provides a structured, end-to-end path that builds deep understanding by implementing algorithms from scratch before using frameworks, and ensures every lesson produces a practical, shippable artifact.
llmSummary.whyNow: With 32,318 stars, a 7-day average delta of 405 stars/day, and a 24-hour delta of 605 stars, this repo is experiencing sustained, high-velocity growth. The acceleration ratio of 1.9 indicates strong and consistent momentum, not a sudden spike. The project has attracted over 150,000 readers and 241,000 page views in the last 30 days, signaling broad community adoption.
llmSummary.whatChanged: No single obvious trigger — the project appears to be sustaining organic growth. The README was recently updated (pushed June 14, 2026) and the curriculum continues to expand, but no major release or external event is evident.
llmSummary.whyTrending: The curriculum fills a clear gap: it offers a free, comprehensive, hands-on path from math to production agents, with a unique 'build it, use it, ship it' philosophy. The inclusion of multiple languages (Python, TypeScript, Rust, Julia) and the focus on producing reusable artifacts (prompts, skills, agents, MCP servers) resonates with developers who want deep understanding and practical output.
llmSummary.developerTakeaway: This is a high-quality, structured resource for anyone serious about AI engineering. It's worth cloning and exploring, especially for developers who want to move beyond API wrappers and understand the underlying mechanics.
llmSummary.developerInsight: The project signals a growing demand for deep, foundational AI education that prioritizes understanding over speed. Developers are increasingly seeking resources that teach them to build from scratch, not just consume frameworks. The curriculum's emphasis on shipping artifacts also reflects a shift toward practical, portfolio-building learning.
llmSummary.targetUsers: Developers and engineers who want to learn AI engineering from the ground up, from math to production. Suitable for beginners with coding experience and experienced practitioners looking to fill gaps in their understanding.
llmSummary.riskNotes: The curriculum is massive (503 lessons, ~320 hours) and may be overwhelming. Some lessons may be incomplete or in progress. The project is maintained by a single creator, so long-term sustainability depends on continued contributions and community support.

projects.name: agent-skills

llmSummary.problemSolved: AI coding agents often produce low-quality, inconsistent code because they lack the structured workflows, quality gates, and best practices that senior engineers follow. This repo encodes those practices into reusable skill files that agents can execute consistently.
llmSummary.whyNow: The repo is experiencing explosive growth with 58,952 stars, a 24-hour delta of 1,919, and a 7-day delta of 10,228. The three-day average star delta is 2,396.7, and the acceleration ratio is 0.8 (high but not sudden breakout). This sustained hot trend reflects massive developer interest in standardizing AI agent behavior for production code.
llmSummary.whatChanged: No single obvious trigger — the project appears to be sustaining organic growth. The high velocity and consistent star accumulation over the past week suggest broad adoption driven by word-of-mouth and the growing need for agentic coding workflows.
llmSummary.whyTrending: As AI coding agents (Claude Code, Cursor, Copilot) become mainstream, developers need a way to enforce consistent, high-quality engineering practices. This repo provides a ready-made, open-source solution that works across multiple agent platforms, filling a critical gap in the AI-assisted development stack.
llmSummary.developerTakeaway: This is a practical, immediately usable resource for any developer using AI coding agents. It offers 24 structured skills (slash commands) that can be installed in Claude Code, Cursor, Windsurf, Gemini CLI, and others, turning agents into disciplined engineering teammates.
llmSummary.developerInsight: The rapid adoption signals a shift: developers are moving from 'prompting agents' to 'governing agents' with structured workflows. This repo is a blueprint for how agent behavior can be standardized, and it may influence how future AI coding tools design their skill systems.
llmSummary.targetUsers: Software engineers and teams using AI coding agents (Claude Code, Cursor, Copilot, Windsurf, Gemini CLI) who want to enforce production-grade engineering practices like spec-driven development, TDD, code review, and CI/CD automation.
llmSummary.riskNotes: The skills are Markdown files that rely on the agent's ability to follow instructions consistently. Effectiveness may vary across different agents and models. The project is new and rapidly evolving, so breaking changes or incomplete coverage are possible.

projects.description: DeepSeek-native AI coding agent for your terminal. Engineered around prefix-cache stability — leave it running.

llmSummary.oneLiner: A DeepSeek-native AI coding agent for the terminal, rewritten in Go for prefix-cache stability and low token costs.
llmSummary.problemSolved: Developers need a persistent, config-driven AI coding assistant that leverages DeepSeek's prefix caching to reduce costs during long sessions, while supporting multiple models, plugins, and a sandboxed environment.
llmSummary.whyNow: With 21,996 stars, a 24-hour delta of +390, and a 7-day average of +436 stars per day, this project shows sustained high-velocity growth. The acceleration ratio of 0.9 indicates a steady, organic climb rather than a sudden spike, making it a reliable signal of developer interest.
llmSummary.whatChanged: The 1.0 ground-up rewrite in Go (from TypeScript) was recently released, as noted in the README. This major version change likely attracted attention and migration from legacy users, contributing to the sustained star growth.
llmSummary.whyTrending: It combines a hot topic (AI coding agents) with a specific optimization for DeepSeek's prefix cache, offering a practical cost-saving advantage. The Go rewrite promises better performance and a single binary distribution, appealing to developers seeking lightweight, cross-platform tools.
llmSummary.developerTakeaway: This project demonstrates the value of optimizing for a specific LLM's caching mechanism to reduce costs, and the shift to Go highlights the trend toward compiled, dependency-free AI tools.
llmSummary.developerInsight: For AI developers, this signals a growing demand for specialized, cost-efficient coding agents that integrate deeply with a single provider's strengths (like DeepSeek's prefix cache). The plugin architecture and multi-model support also point to a modular future for AI tooling.
llmSummary.targetUsers: Developers using DeepSeek models who want a terminal-based AI coding assistant with persistent sessions, plugin extensibility, and low token costs.
llmSummary.riskNotes: Heavy reliance on DeepSeek's API and prefix caching; changes to DeepSeek's pricing or caching behavior could impact the project's value proposition. The high number of open issues (483) may indicate growing pains or community demand outpacing development.

llmSummary.problemSolved: Most AI agents are either too heavy, closed-source, or tied to a single provider. nanobot gives you a small, readable core that you can extend with your own tools, chat channels, and models, all while keeping your data and workflows under your control.

llmSummary.whyTrending: nanobot combines the appeal of a lightweight, open-source AI agent with practical features for real-world use: a built-in WebUI, support for multiple chat platforms (Telegram, Discord, WeChat, etc.), MCP integration, and a growing list of LLM providers. aihotHighlights.title: 白宫AI监管决定被指偏袒OpenAI与亚马逊
aihotHighlights.summary: 白宫周五做出的AI监管决定被指偏袒OpenAI、亚马逊等企业，同时对Anthropic施压不足24小时，缺乏透明度和事实依据。Gary Marcus、Dean W Ball及卡托研究所Kevin Frazier等专家指出，这种由少数人闭门快速决策的做法带有腐败嫌疑，可能促使其他国家加速发展"主权AI"甚至中国AI，并导致美国人才流失。Anthropic声明称政府应在法定程序中基于技术事实阻止不安全部署，而非当前方式。Marcus呼吁建立独立机构负责AI监管，确保公平、清晰、基于证据的执行。

aihotHighlights.title: Satya Nadella：没有生态的前沿不稳定
aihotHighlights.summary: 微软CEO Satya Nadella认为，AI驱动的平台转变首次实现人与数字系统间的认知循环。企业需同时构建人力资本（知识、判断、关系）与token资本（自有的AI能力），且人力资本不会贬值，反而随token资本增长而增值。真正的机会在于建立人力资本与token资本复合增长的学习循环--企业应能替换通用模型而不丢失已内化的专家知识，通过私有评估和强化学习让模型从内部真实轨迹中持续提升。他警告，若所有价值被少数模型吞噬，将重演全球化空心化悲剧，呼吁构建前沿生态系统，让每家企业、行业和国家拥有自己的学习循环。

items.title: 白宫AI监管决定被指偏袒OpenAI与亚马逊
items.description: 白宫周五做出的AI监管决定被指偏袒OpenAI、亚马逊等企业，同时对Anthropic施压不足24小时，缺乏透明度和事实依据。Gary Marcus、Dean W Ball及卡托研究所Kevin Frazier等专家指出，这种由少数人闭门快速决策的做法带有腐败嫌疑，可能促使其他国家加速发展"主权AI"甚至中国AI，并导致美国人才流失。Anthropic声明称政府应在法定程序中基于技术事实阻止不安全部署，而非当前方式。Marcus呼吁建立独立机构负责AI监管，确保公平、清晰、基于证据的执行。

items.title: Satya Nadella：没有生态的前沿不稳定
items.description: 微软CEO Satya Nadella认为，AI驱动的平台转变首次实现人与数字系统间的认知循环。企业需同时构建人力资本（知识、判断、关系）与token资本（自有的AI能力），且人力资本不会贬值，反而随token资本增长而增值。真正的机会在于建立人力资本与token资本复合增长的学习循环--企业应能替换通用模型而不丢失已内化的专家知识，通过私有评估和强化学习让模型从内部真实轨迹中持续提升。他警告，若所有价值被少数模型吞噬，将重演全球化空心化悲剧，呼吁构建前沿生态系统，让每家企业、行业和国家拥有自己的学习循环。

items.description: 微软CEO Satya Nadella认为，AI驱动的平台转变首次实现人与数字系统间的认知循环。企业需同时构建人力资本（知识、判断、关系）与token资本（自有的AI能力），且人力资本不会贬值，反而随token资本增长而增值。真正的机会在于建立人力资本与token资本复合增长的学习循环--企业应能替换通用模型而不丢失已内化的专家知识，通过私有评估和强化学习让模型从内部真实轨迹中持续提升。他警告，若所有价值被少数模型吞噬，将重演全球化空心化悲剧，呼吁构建前沿生态系统，让每家企业、行业和国家拥有自己的学习循环。

items.title: 乔木小说创作 Skill 开源发布
items.description: 开源乔木小说创作 Skill，用户只需说"我想写一个小说"或指定风格，AI 自动生成剧情梗概、人物设定、钩子、经典桥段、人物欲望、冲突升级和结尾。与 AI 讨论确认后，可生成完整、低 AI 味的小说。安装命令：npx skills add joeseesun/qiaomu-novel-generator，Github 开源地址见评论区。

llmSummary.riskNotes: The star spike is likely driven by social media attention, not proven production adoption. Compression quality and latency trade-offs need evaluation. No evidence of widespread usage beyond initial hype.

sourceItems.summary: 白宫周五做出的AI监管决定被指偏袒OpenAI、亚马逊等企业，同时对Anthropic施压不足24小时，缺乏透明度和事实依据。Gary Marcus、Dean W Ball及卡托研究所Kevin Frazier等专家指出，这种由少数人闭门快速决策的做法带有腐败嫌疑，可能促使其他国家加速发展"主权AI"甚至中国AI，并导致美国人才流失。Anthropic声明称政府应在法定程序中基于技术事实阻止不安全部署，而非当前方式。Marcus呼吁建立独立机构负责AI监管，确保公平、清晰、基于证据的执行。

trendEntities.title: Satya Nadella：没有生态的前沿不稳定
normalizedKeys: title:satya nadella 没有生态的前沿不稳定

items.title: Satya Nadella：没有生态的前沿不稳定
items.summary: 微软CEO Satya Nadella认为，AI驱动的平台转变首次实现人与数字系统间的认知循环。企业需同时构建人力资本（知识、判断、关系）与token资本（自有的AI能力），且人力资本不会贬值，反而随token资本增长而增值。真正的机会在于建立人力资本与token资本复合增长的学习循环--企业应能替换通用模型而不丢失已内化的专家知识，通过私有评估和强化学习让模型从内部真实轨迹中持续提升。他警告，若所有价值被少数模型吞噬，将重演全球化空心化悲剧，呼吁构建前沿生态系统，让每家企业、行业和国家拥有自己的学习循环。

trendEntities.title: 谷歌Android安全负责人因反对军事AI合作辞职
normalizedKeys: title:谷歌android安全负责人因反对军事ai合作辞职

items.title: 谷歌Android安全负责人因反对军事AI合作辞职
items.summary: 谷歌Android平台安全负责人René Mayrhofer辞职，他在5月18日内部告别信中指责公司"丧失道德指针"，批评谷歌悄悄放弃碳中和目标（因AI模型能耗），并与美国战争部签署允许AI用于"任何合法目的"的协议。今年4月下旬谷歌宣布向五角大楼提供AI用于机密工作，2025年2月更新AI原则时移除了不使用AI开发武器或监控工具的承诺。Mayrhofer担忧谷歌AI产品可能被用于针对公民的大规模监控，包括自己和家人。

normalizedKeys: title:扎克伯格承认 meta ai 转型 脱轨 裁员 10 转岗 7000 人后组织调整过快

items.title: 扎克伯格承认 Meta AI 转型"脱轨"：裁员 10%、转岗 7000 人后组织调整过快
items.summary: 路透社披露的 Meta 内部备忘录显示，CEO 扎克伯格承认公司在 AI 转型中组织调整节奏过快，带来员工安置、管理跨度等问题，预计未来"几乎肯定会犯更多错误"。Meta 今年 5 月已裁减全球 10% 员工，并将 7000 人转入 AI 相关新项目。为缓解协作问题，公司将增加团队建设预算，7 月举办黑客松。新成立的应用 AI 工程部门个人贡献者与管理者比例最高达 50：1。扎克伯格重申今年不再全公司裁员。

sourceItems.summary: 路透社披露的 Meta 内部备忘录显示，CEO 扎克伯格承认公司在 AI 转型中组织调整节奏过快，带来员工安置、管理跨度等问题，预计未来"几乎肯定会犯更多错误"。Meta 今年 5 月已裁减全球 10% 员工，并将 7000 人转入 AI 相关新项目。为缓解协作问题，公司将增加团队建设预算，7 月举办黑客松。新成立的应用 AI 工程部门个人贡献者与管理者比例最高达 50：1。扎克伯格重申今年不再全公司裁员。

items.title: 智谱 GLM-5.2 全量开放，支持 1M 上下文且下周开源
items.summary: GLM-5.2 是智谱迄今能力最强的开源模型，支持真正可用的 1M 上下文，在长程任务中继续保持领先，并被智谱称为最强的国产 Coding 模型。今晚 5：21 起面向 GLM Coding Plan 全量用户开放（覆盖 Lite、Pro、Max、团队版）。API 将于下周上线，模型下周正式开源，遵循 MIT 协议。

trendEntities.title: SemiAnalysis 洞察 Token 经济：200 美元 AI 订阅榨出 70 倍用量
normalizedKeys: title:semianalysis 洞察 token 经济 200 美元 ai 订阅榨出 70 倍用量

items.title: SemiAnalysis 洞察 Token 经济：200 美元 AI 订阅榨出 70 倍用量
items.summary: SemiAnalysis 购买了 Anthropic 和 OpenAI 的全部订阅方案，模拟高强度编码任务直至触及每周上限。月费 200 美元的 Claude Max 20x 方案，按 API 价格换算最高可消耗约值 8000 美元的 token；ChatGPT Pro 20x 方案对应最高约值 14000 美元的 token。用户通过订阅可获取 40 至 70 倍的 API 价值，该机构指出这种价格体系在重度用户持续榨满上限后可能难以长期维持。

trendEntities.title: Oran Ge 开源《人味儿写作心法.skill》解决AI写作缺人味
normalizedKeys: title:oran ge 开源 人味儿写作心法 skill 解决ai写作缺人味

items.title: Oran Ge 开源《人味儿写作心法.skill》解决AI写作缺人味
items.summary: Oran Ge 让 Claude Fable 5 打磨文案三遍，发现改稿越来越讲究却缺"人味儿"。他与 AI 讨论后得出结论：人写的文字背后有"存在感"--作者在具体位置付出过具体代价，而 AI 无法复现。为此他制作了《人味儿写作心法.skill》，专用于自写文章或口述后让 AI 改稿的场景，旨在保留文字的人味。该技能已开源免费发布在 GitHub。

items.title: 今年价值1300亿美元的数据中心项目因抗议被叫停

trendEntities.title: olmo-eval：面向模型开发循环的评估工作台
normalizedKeys: title:olmo eval 面向模型开发循环的评估工作台

items.title: olmo-eval：面向模型开发循环的评估工作台
items.summary: olmo-eval 是基于 OLMES 标准构建的评估工作台，专为 LLM 持续开发中的反复评测场景设计。相比 OLMES，它减少了新增评测的实现工作量，支持 agentic 和多轮评测作为一等用例，并允许根据基准需求选择轻量直接运行或容器化隔离运行。采用模块化架构，模型、工具、容器环境、辅助模型均可独立替换。评测结果同时报告分数、标准误差和最小可检测效应。与 Harbor 侧重于发布不同，olmo-eval 聚焦开发阶段快速迭代，可逐问题对比检查点输出以区分真实改进与噪声。

items.title: 字节豆包上线"任务模式"：支持定时执行与文件生成，"思考模式"升级为"专家模式"
items.summary: 6月12日，字节跳动旗下AI应用豆包大范围上线"任务模式"，支持定时执行、零代码网页生成、一键PPT生成、数据可视化分析等全链路Agent执行。原"思考模式"升级为"专家模式"，调用豆包大模型2.0 Pro版本，强化深度推理能力。App顶部模式切换改为"快速、专家、任务"。基础功能免费，高阶服务付费，专业版三档：标准版68元/月或688元/年，加强版200元/月或2048元/年，专业版500元/月或5088元/年。

items.title: MiniMax M3 开源权重模型发布，已上架 HuggingFace
items.summary: MiniMax 发布开源权重模型 M3，约 428B 总参数、23B 激活参数，已上传 HuggingFace。该模型融合三种前沿能力：编码与智能体方面达 59.0% SWE-Bench Pro、66.0% Terminal Bench 2.1、34.8% SWE-fficiency、28.8% KernelBench Hard、74.2% MCP Atlas；采用 MiniMax 稀疏注意力将上下文窗口扩展至 1M token；原生多模态。同步上线 MiniMax Code 工具及 API 平台。权重与技术报告预计约 10 天后发布。

items.title: Kimi 发布并开源最新代码模型 Kimi-K2.7-Code
items.summary: Kimi 发布并开源最新代码模型 Kimi-K2.7-Code。相比 K2.6，其在 Kimi Code Bench v2 上提升 +21.8%，Program Bench 提升 +11.0%，MLS Bench Lite 提升 +31.5%。推理效率改进，推理 token 使用量降低 30%，长时编码任务中指令遵循和端到端成功率均提升。6x 高速模式即将推出，即日起可通过 Kimi API 和 Kimi Code 使用。

trendEntities.title: inclusionAI 发布 VISTA-4B GUI 定位视觉语言模型
normalizedKeys: title:inclusionai 发布 vista 4b gui 定位视觉语言模型

items.title: inclusionAI 发布 VISTA-4B GUI 定位视觉语言模型
items.summary: VISTA-4B 是基于 Qwen3.5-4B 骨干的 GUI 定位模型，输入截图与自然语言指令，输出归一化 0-1000 坐标。训练采用视图一致 GRPO 和自验证交叉视图锚定。在 GUI 定位基准上，SSPro 得分 64.2（相比 GRPO-4B 提升 2.0），SSV2 得分 93.8（下降 0.4），OSWorld-G 得分 61.2（提升 1.3），OSWorld-G-R 得分 69.7（提升 0.5）。模型已开源在 HuggingFace，推荐使用提示词并返回 【x，y】 格式坐标。

items.title: olmo-eval：面向模型开发循环的评估工作台
items.summary: olmo-eval 是基于 OLMES 标准构建的评估工作台，专为 LLM 持续开发中的反复评测场景设计。相比 OLMES，它减少了新增评测的实现工作量，支持 agentic 和多轮评测作为一等用例，并允许根据基准需求选择轻量直接运行或容器化隔离运行。采用模块化架构，模型、工具、容器环境、辅助模型均可独立替换。评测结果同时报告分数、标准误差和最小可检测效应。与 Harbor 侧重于发布不同，olmo-eval 聚焦开发阶段快速迭代，可逐问题对比检查点输出以区分真实改进与噪声。

items.title: MiniMax M3 开源权重模型发布，已上架 HuggingFace
items.summary: MiniMax 发布开源权重模型 M3，约 428B 总参数、23B 激活参数，已上传 HuggingFace。该模型融合三种前沿能力：编码与智能体方面达 59.0% SWE-Bench Pro、66.0% Terminal Bench 2.1、34.8% SWE-fficiency、28.8% KernelBench Hard、74.2% MCP Atlas；采用 MiniMax 稀疏注意力将上下文窗口扩展至 1M token；原生多模态。同步上线 MiniMax Code 工具及 API 平台。权重与技术报告预计约 10 天后发布。
